title: SSH Brute Force Response
summary: Response steps for a burst of failed SSH/RDP logins indicating password guessing, spraying, or credential stuffing against one or more accounts.
steps: Identify the source IP(s) generating the failed authentication attempts and the targeted account(s).
Block the offending source IP at the host firewall or perimeter.
Confirm whether any attempt succeeded; if so, treat the account as potentially compromised.
Enforce or verify rate limiting and lockout policy on the affected service.
Require a password reset for targeted accounts and recommend MFA.

title: Account Compromise Response
summary: Response steps when a legitimate account is believed to be compromised, for example a successful login following a brute force burst or a login from a malicious source.
steps: Disable or suspend the affected account immediately to stop further access.
Terminate the account's active sessions and revoke active tokens or keys.
Reset the account credentials and require re-enrollment of MFA.
Review the account's recent activity for privilege escalation, lateral movement, or persistence.
Block the malicious source IP(s) associated with the compromise.

title: Privilege Escalation Response
summary: Response steps when a user unexpectedly escalates privileges, such as first-time sudo to root shortly after login or abuse of a setuid binary.
steps: Confirm whether the privilege escalation was authorized for that user and host.
If unauthorized, disable the account and isolate the host from the network.
Review sudo, auth, and command history for actions taken with elevated privileges.
Check for new users, cron jobs, SSH keys, or services created for persistence.
Escalate to human review before destructive remediation on privileged accounts.

title: File Integrity Violation Response
summary: Response steps for unauthorized changes to monitored files or directories detected by file integrity monitoring.
steps: Identify the changed file(s), the type of change, and the host affected.
Determine whether the change corresponds to an approved change window.
If unauthorized, snapshot the affected files and compare against a known-good baseline.
Restore legitimate content from backup and investigate the responsible process.
Check for related indicators such as new binaries or backdoors.

title: Data Exfiltration Response
summary: Response steps when data appears to be leaving the environment over an unexpected channel or in unusual volume.
steps: Identify the source host, destination, protocol, and volume of the suspicious transfer.
Block the destination and the exfiltration channel at the perimeter.
Isolate the source host to prevent further data loss.
Determine what data was accessed and assess sensitivity and regulatory impact.
Disable the responsible account if malicious and engage incident response.

title: Network Anomaly / Reconnaissance Response
summary: Response steps for scanning or reconnaissance activity against internal assets, such as port sweeps or service enumeration from a single source.
steps: Identify the scanning source and the assets/ports being probed.
Block the scanning source IP at the perimeter.
Increase monitoring on the targeted hosts for follow-on exploitation.
Review exposed services for unnecessary attack surface.
Correlate with any subsequent authentication or exploitation attempts.

title: Data Destruction Response
summary: Response steps for mass file deletion, disk wiping, or destruction of backups indicating sabotage or ransomware impact.
steps: Isolate affected hosts immediately to halt ongoing destruction.
Identify the scope of destroyed or encrypted data and whether backups are intact.
Disable the account and terminate the process performing the destruction.
Preserve forensic evidence before any restoration.
Restore data from the most recent clean backup and validate integrity.
